stories.headline: French Socialists to Oppose Moulin as Bank of France Head
stories.summary: Socialist lawmakers will oppose Emmanuel Macron’s nominee for Bank of France Governor, raising the risks of a political blow for the French president and uncertainty over the composition of the European Central Bank’s Governing Council.

stories.headline: Target’s Stock Rally Raises Earnings Bar as Comeback Gains Steam
stories.summary: Target Corp. has quietly emerged as one of this year’s hottest retail stocks, outperforming many staples and big-box peers. The rally has also raised the bar heading into quarterly results.

stories.headline: Record Number of Borrowers Are Selling Bonds in Europe’s Market
stories.summary: Borrowers are pouring into Europe’s bond market at the fastest pace ever, locking in funding before the region’s central bank is likely to hike interest rates.

stories.headline: Japan, South Korea Deepen Ties Amid Global Tensions
stories.summary:
Japan’s Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi and South Korean President Lee Jae Myung pledged closer cooperation on energy and security, while strengthening ties with other allies as geopolitical tensions continue to rise.

stories.headline: Agnico Eagle to Build $1.7 Billion Gold Mine in Canada’s Arctic
stories.summary: Agnico Eagle Mines Ltd. is moving ahead with a large gold mine in Canada’s far north, a project being hailed by Prime Minister Mark Carney’s government as an anchor for economic development in the strategically important Arctic region.

stories.headline: Cholamandalam to Sell Largest Floater Bond By Indian Lender
stories.summary: Shadow lender Cholamandalam Investment and Finance Co. will raise 50 billion rupees ($518 million) through a floating-rate bond, according to people familiar with the matter, the largest such local currency debt sale by an Indian financier.

stories.headline: Brazil Sends Cheap Soy Oil Abroad as Biodiesel Plan Stalls
stories.summary: The world’s top soybean producer Brazil is processing record amounts of the crop, but a delay in the country’s plans to boost local demand through biofuel mandates has resulted in an excess of cheap soy oil supplies being shipped overseas.
